key_differentials.name: Sarr
key_differentials.why: Crystal Palace attacking mid, sub-8% EO, on set-pieces and a direct goal threat in a home final-day game; a haul swings rank hard against a Haaland-less template.

key_differentials.name: Mateta
key_differentials.why: CRY penalty taker with elite box volume; captained here as a contrarian armband while the field scrambles for a Haaland replacement.

key_differentials.name: Watkins
key_differentials.why: AVL penalty + big-chance share; genuine sub-8% striker with real haul ceiling on the final day.

notes: FINAL-GW max-points build, adjusted for team news. The standard sim-lab/intel anchor (captain Haaland) is OFF the table: GW38 reporting has Haaland rested (Pep's last game), Saka benched, and Gabriel uncertain — so the dominant captain EV (7.17) is no longer live and the field's armband collapses onto a tiny pool. I attack that gap rather than fight over it. CAPTAIN MATETA: Crystal Palace at home, on penalties, with high penalty-box volume — a sub-15% contrarian armband while template managers scramble. Double Palace attack (Mateta + Sarr) is the punt engine; Sarr is the key sub-8% differential. Palmer (vice) and Mbeumo are the reliable nailed-on midfield floor; João Pedro, Semenyo and Watkins add further low-template attacking upside. Defence is a Liverpool/Bournemouth/Palace clean-sheet lottery kept cheap to fund the attack. Avoided the rotation-risk ARS/MCI assets entirely (no Haaland, Saka benched). xpts.json was all-zero (data gap) and sim-lab assumed a Haaland start, so projections are reconstructed bottom-up from role + fixture + ownership priors: Mateta ~4.8 base (captain doubles to ~9.6), Palmer ~5.0, Mbeumo ~4.6, Semenyo ~4.4, João Pedro ~4.5, Watkins ~4.6, Sarr ~4.0, plus the defence/keeper CS expectation. Projected XI total ~69 xPts incl. captain. High-variance by design — chasing rank, not protecting it.
